entities.name: SORGHUM
entities.description: A primary cereal crop used for food, feed, and fuel, serving as a model for functional genetics and genomics of tropical grasses

entities.name: FUNCTIONAL GENETICS
entities.description: The study of the relationship between genes and their phenotypes in organisms

entities.name: GENOMICS
entities.description: The study of the complete set of DNA within an organism

entities.name: BIOTIC STRESS
entities.description: Stress caused by living organisms that negatively impacts agricultural production

entities.name: ABIOTIC STRESS
entities.description: Stress caused by non-living environmental factors that negatively impacts agricultural production

entities.name: HIGH-YIELD
entities.description: The ability of a crop to produce a high volume of agricultural output

entities.name: DISEASE-RESISTANCE
entities.description: The ability of a crop to resist biological diseases

entities.name: CLIMATE-RESILIENCE
entities.description: The ability of a crop to withstand and recover from climate-related stresses

entities.name: MARKER-ASSISTED BREEDING
entities.description: A breeding strategy using genetic markers to develop cultivars with desired traits

entities.name: GENETIC MARKERS
entities.description: DNA sequences used to identify specific locations on a chromosome to assist in selection

entities.name: MOLECULAR PLANT BREEDING
entities.description: The application of molecular biology tools to improve plant breeding

entities.name: GENETICS
entities.description: The scientific study of genes, genetic variation, and heredity

entities.name: GENOMIC SELECTION
entities.description: A method of predicting breeding values using genome-wide markers

entities.name: GENOME EDITING
entities.description: A technique for making precise, targeted changes to the genome of an organism

entities.name: MARKER-ASSISTED SELECTION
entities.description: A process of selecting individuals based on the presence of specific genetic markers

entities.name: PLANT BREEDING PROCESS
entities.description: The overall process of developing new crop varieties
